  <h2 id="lecture_heading" class="section-title dsp-flex-xs flex-align-items-center-xs" data-lecture-id="30899936" data-next-lecture-id="31997861" data-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/30899936" data-next-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/31997861" ,="" data-previous-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/16636509" data-previous-lecture-id="16636509">

    10.How To Process Your Crazy Irrational Feelings
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=2 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=54601685, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-54601685">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">As an artist, surely you know...</span></p><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">There will be ups and downs.</span></p><p>Sometime you feel like you can even set an alarm to it.</p><p>Yet somehow, every low always feels so original.</p><p>Like it's the first time it's ever happened.</p><p>Do you have a strategy for handling those irrational feelings when they arrive?</p><p>You should have one for anything that you can count on reoccurring regularly. </p><p>It's good for business. </p><p>It's good for play.</p><p>No matter the emotions, there are two things every creative must learn to do with all those crazy feelings you get.</p><p>And you need to do these 2 things AT THE SAME TIME.</p><p>AT THE SAME TIME is key.</p><p>Here they are...</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>1. HONOR YOUR FEELINGS</strong></h1><p>As a creative, your feelings are part of expressing your job. You'd be crazy not to put them on a pedestal. </p><p>You are an emotion machine. </p><p>Feel those feelings for all they're worth. Understand where they came from. How unique they are. </p><p>New feelings especially. That's a special breed of emotion that has arrived to teach you something major.</p><p>The bonus?</p><p>These emotions may give you great art one day. If not in the next hour...</p><p>You'll be talking about them in interviews and on stage accepting awards some day. To recognize their worth immediately.</p><p>Even crazy irrational feelings have a place in your life.</p><p>Especially if you're hot-blooded. </p><p>You may be the type to switch up feelings because you're bored of the latest ones you've been serving up.</p><p>Feel all those fiery emotions and express the full range of who you are.</p><p>But...</p><p>Because you're also a high-output creative (or aspire to be), there's something else you have to do.</p><p>Often within the same hour.</p><p>Something that "the normals" will never understand:</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>2. IGNORE THE FEELINGS</strong></h1><p>I heard Krishnamurti tell this cool little story on YouTube...</p><p>"Once, there was a farmer. One of his sons found a horse. His neighbors said he was lucky. The farmer said, "Maybe." The next day, the son was attempting to train the horse, and he fell and broke his leg. The farmer's neighbor said he was unlucky. The farmer said, "Maybe." The next day the country went to war, and the army came by and took his other two sons. They both died in that war. The son with the broken leg healed. His neighbors said he was lucky. The farmer said, "Maybe.""</p><p>I don't know if what you're going through today will end up being "the worst" moment of your life or "the best".</p><p>But I DO know that if you stay focused on your daily duty as "a creator of beautiful things",... That your self-esteem won't have to suffer. Even if other things are upside-down at the moment.</p><p>You can be down AND STILL feel 100% blessed.</p><p>You can be hella mad at your mom for constantly triggering your darkest impulses AND STILL wake up at 5 a.m... Before your dentist appointment... To finish a new ground-breaking painting.</p><p>Your current government job may make you cry in the parking lot once a month... AND you can STILL be focused enough to make all the baller moves with your crew every weekend. Those green screen videos might get out of this hellhole by next year.</p><p>Embrace all these contradictory thoughts.</p><p>That's what it means to be a creative.</p><p>Feeling a range of things that others don't... Or are afraid to.</p><p>You're emotional because you're unsure.</p><p>But even when you're unsure, your limbs still work. Use those limbs to make a few moves that put the odds further in your favor today.</p><p>Narrow the gap between the vision in your mind and the dire reality you're facing right now. </p><p>Do one action that will put a half-smile on your face before you go back to feeling down in the dumps.</p><p>Be the walking contradiction most creatives are. </p><p>Like, how can you be so productive, making all this art and making all these moves, while you're an emotional wreck?</p><p>I dunno.</p><p>Ask FKA Twigs.</p><p>Ask Nine Ince Nails.</p><p>Ask Post Malone.</p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/27885125" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">"Passion" is all about what you're feeling that day. But "obsession" knows it's got a job to do either way.</a></p><p>Let yourself feel what you gotta feel, but give yourself permission to suspend it for the moments you need to make G.O.A.T. moves. </p><p>Keep to your self-discipline schedule. "Do the artistic work that needs to be done". It's an amazing way to blunt the negative impact of unruly thoughts.</p><p>"IGNORE THE FEELINGS" doesn't mean you don't deal with the emotions later on. They must be dealt with. They must be honored. "IGNORE THE FEELINGS" means you don't let it stop you from doing what must be done today.</p><p>Remember... You're an artist with a message for the world... That doesn't suddenly change because you had a fight with boo.</p><p>Don't cave into moments you don't understand the full impact of when you can be doing things NOW to influence what those things will mean in the future.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>But that includes ignoring the "good feelings" too!</strong></h1><p>The good feelings are the ones that get the ego thinking you can take a day off. Or that you can start slacking. Noooooooo...</p><p>The good feelings will make you feel like the "bad feelings" will never happen again. And that you got things under control. </p><p>Don't fall for it.</p><p>Nothing could be further from the truth.</p><p>Humans were designed to go back and forth between feeling good and feeling bad. Like, for the rest of our lives.</p><p>Postpartum depression… The comedown… They are all essential parts of the human experience.</p><p>But luckily, we also a certain amount of power to influence HOW LONG we linger in each phase. </p><p>There is no reason not to have a grand life with LONG periods of feeling energized, love, and grateful... And SHORT periods of self-doubt and sadness.</p><p>Start by practicing this whole "putting your emotions on hold -- feel them later" G.O.A.T strategy. </p><p>Human beings have the unique ability to change how they feel in an instant if they choose. Not for an extended time, necessarily. But long enough to get a few things done.</p><p>That's how professional artists and performers can do their job no matter what "bad news" they got or what "irrational feelings" have crept up.</p><p>They put those feelings on hold when it's game time. You should start practicing this skill now since you're going to be a star one day.</p><p>I'd suggest you get intimate with the concept.</p><p>The next time you're feeling like shit on a day that was supposed to be productive... Put the feelings on hold. Suspend your knee-jerk reaction to always cave-in to every emotion you feel.</p><p>Do what you said you'd do today.</p><p>Your actions today are the raw materials for your feelings tomorrow.</p><p>Then after you've done your work... If you still feel like it, indulge in overthinking and feeling bad over a big bowl of peanut butter chocolate ice-cream.</p><p>And fucking cry, cry, cry...</p><h2><strong><em>"Feelings, like the weather, should be revered or ignored." Tom Robbins</em></strong></h2><p></p>
